KAUA‘I COUNTY COUNCIL

MEETING NOTICE AND AGENDA

WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 18, 2026

8:30 AM OR SOON THEREAFTER

COUNCIL CHAMBERS, HISTORIC COUNTY BUILDING

4396 RICE STREET, SUITE 201

LīHUʻE KAUAʻI, HAWAIʻI 96766

AND

MEETING ROOM 106, HISTORIC COUNTY BUILDING

4396 RICE STREET, FIRST FLOOR, LīHUʻE KAUAʻI, HAWAIʻI 96766

MEETING INFORMATION:

This is an in-person meeting at multiple meeting sites connected by interactive conference

technology pursuant to Hawai‘i Revised Statutes (HRS) Section 92-3.5. Councilmembers will

attend the meeting at the Council Chambers location only. Members of the public may attend the

meeting at either location.

Oral Testimony: Accepted in-person at the Council Chambers and Meeting Room 106.

Written Testimony: May be submitted for any meeting via: email to

counciltestimony@kauai.gov; fax to (808) 241-6349; mail to the Office of the County Clerk,

Council Services Division at 4396 Rice Street, Suite 209, Lihu‘e, Hawai‘i, 96766; or hand- delivery at Suite 209 and Meeting Room 106 during regular business hours (7:45 a.m. to 4:30

p.m., Monday through Friday, excluding holidays).

Courtesy Livestream, Video, and Minutes: Meeting livestream (recorded video to follow) is

available at www.kauai.gov/Government/Council/Webcast-Meetings. If the courtesy livestream

is unavailable for any reason, the meeting will proceed. Meeting minutes will be completed

pursuant to HRS Chapter 92 and electronically posted at www.kauai.gov/Government/Council

following Council approval.

Courtesy Remote Oral Testimony: This is not a remote meeting pursuant to HRS Section 92-3.7.

The option of remote oral testimony is offered as a courtesy only. To register, testifiers shall sign

up via emailing remote.signup@kauai.gov, indicating the agenda item(s) they would like to

provide testimony on. Emails are due no later than 12:00 Noon the business day prior to the

meeting. Those who timely register will receive remote testimony access and instructions via

reply email. Those wishing to view the meeting but not provide testimony will be directed to the

courtesy livestream (via the website link above). If the courtesy remote testimony access is

unavailable for any reason, the meeting will proceed.

Additional Note for Both Methods of Oral Testimony: Generally, each agenda item is addressed

(and oral testimony received) in the order listed in the agenda; however, agenda items may occur

out of order without notice and oral testimony is not accepted for completed agenda items. Those

seeking the guaranteed ability to testify should report: (1) in-person, and (2) by the meeting start

time.

For Further Assistance: If you need an auxiliary aid/service, other accommodation due to a

disability, or an interpreter for non-English speaking persons, please contact the Office of the

County Clerk, Council Services Division at (808) 241-4188 or cokcouncil@kauai.gov as soon as

possible. Requests made as early as possible will allow adequate time to fulfill your request.

Upon request, this notice is available in alternate formats such as large print, braille, or electronic

COUNTY COUNCIL MEETING AGENDA FEBRUARY 18, 2026

1

Page 2 of 6

A. MEETING CALLED TO ORDER.

B. ROLL CALL.

C. APPROVAL OF AGENDA.

D. MINUTES of the following meetings of the Council:

1. January 22, 2026 Council Meeting

2. February 4, 2026 Council Meeting

E. CONSENT CALENDAR:

1. C 2026-32 Communication (01/14/2026) from the Mayor, transmitting for Council

consideration and confirmation, the appointment of Dan V. Giovanni (Business) to the

Planning Commission – Partial term ending 12/31/2027. (See Resolution No. 2026-03)

2. C 2026-33 Communication (01/26/2026) from the Housing Director, transmitting for Council

consideration, a Resolution Approving Modifications To The Preliminary Subdivision Map

For The Lima Ola Workforce Housing Project As Provided For In Resolution No. 2016-53,

Approving The Lima Ola Workforce Housing Project Pursuant To Section 201H-38, Hawai‘i

Revised Statutes, And Amending The List Of Approved Statutory Exemptions Approved

Therein. (See Resolution No. 2026-04)

3. C 2026-34 Communication (01/27/2026) from the County Engineer, transmitting for Council

consideration, a Resolution Authorizing Installation Of Speed Tables On Hauiki Road,

Kawaihau District, County Of Kaua‘i. (See Resolution No. 2026-05)

4. C 2026-35 Communication (02/04/2026) from Councilmember Bulosan and Councilmember

Carvalho, transmitting for Council consideration, a Resolution Urging The Hawai‘i State

Legislature To Ensure The Long-Term Sustainability And Funding Of Public, Educational,

And Governmental (PEG) Access Media In Hawai‘i. (See Resolution No. 2026-06)

5. C 2026-36 Communication (02/04/2026) from the Director of Finance, transmitting for

Council information, Periods 1 through 3 Financial Reports – Statement of Revenues,

Statement of Expenditures and Encumbrances, Revenue Report, and Detailed Budget Report

copy.

ADDITIONAL NOTIFICATION – NO ELEVATOR ACCESS TO SECOND FLOOR:

At the time of this meeting notice and agenda posting, the Historic County Building elevator is

temporarily inoperable. Because the Council Chambers is on the second floor of the building, an in- person meeting site at Meeting Room 106 will be connected to the in-person meeting site at the Council

Chambers by interactive conference technology. If audio communication to the meeting cannot be

maintained between the locations, the meeting shall be automatically recessed to restore

communication. If the audio communication cannot be restored within thirty (30) minutes, the meeting

will be terminated. If the audiovisual communication can be restored, the meeting may reconvene. If

the audio communication can be restored, the meeting may reconvene with audio-only communication,

with hardcopies of visual aids made available to all meeting participants pursuant to HRS Section 92-

3.5(c).

COUNTY COUNCIL MEETING AGENDA FEBRUARY 18, 2026

Page 3 of 6

for the period of July 1, 2024 through September 30, 2024, pursuant to Section 11 of

Ordinance No. B-2024-896, relating to the Operating Budget of the County of Kaua‘i for

Fiscal Year 2024-2025. (Copies of the Periods 1 through 3 Financial Reports as of

September 30, 2024 on file in the Of ice of the County Clerk.)

6. C 2026-37 Communication (02/04/2026) from the Director of Finance, transmitting for

Council information, Periods 4 through 6 Financial Reports – Statement of Revenues,

Statement of Expenditures and Encumbrances, Revenue Report, and Detailed Budget Report

for the period of October 1, 2024 through December 31, 2024, pursuant to Section 11 of

Ordinance No. B-2024-896, relating to the Operating Budget of the County of Kaua‘i for

Fiscal Year 2024-2025. (Copies of the Periods 4 through 6 Financial Reports as of

December 31, 2024 on file in the Of ice of the County Clerk.)

7. C 2026-38 Communication (02/04/2026) from the Director of Finance, transmitting for

Council information, Periods 7 through 9 Financial Reports – Statement of Revenues,

Statement of Expenditures and Encumbrances, Revenue Report, and Detailed Budget Report

for the period of January 1, 2025 through March 31, 2025, pursuant to Section 11 of

Ordinance No. B-2024-896, relating to the Operating Budget of the County of Kaua‘i for

Fiscal Year 2024-2025. (Copies of the Periods 7 through 9 Financial Reports as of March

31, 2025 on file in the Of ice of the County Clerk.)

8. C 2026-39 Communication (02/04/2026) from the Director of Finance, transmitting for

Council information, Periods 10 through 12 Financial Reports – Statement of Revenues,

Statement of Expenditures and Encumbrances, Revenue Report, and Detailed Budget Report

for the period of April 1, 2025 through June 30, 2025, pursuant to Section 11 of Ordinance

No. B-2024-896, relating to the Operating Budget of the County of Kaua‘i for Fiscal Year

2024-2025. (Copies of the Periods 10 through 12 Financial Reports as of June 30, 2025 on

file in the Of ice of the County Clerk.)

F. COMMUNICATIONS:

1. C 2026-40 Communication (01/27/2026) from the Housing Director, transmitting for Council

consideration, A Bill For An Ordinance Amending Chapter 6, Kaua‘i County Code 1987, As

Amended, Relating To The County Housing Development Fund. (See Proposed Draft Bill

(No. 2985))

2. C 2026-41 Communication (02/03/2026) from Councilmember Holland, transmitting for

Council consideration, A Bill For An Ordinance Amending Chapter 8, Kaua‘i County Code

1987, As Amended, By Establishing A New Article Relating To Condominium Property

Regimes. (See Proposed Draft Bill (No. 2986))

3. C 2026-42 Communication (02/04/2026) from the Director of Parks & Recreation, requesting

Council approval to purchase unbudgeted equipment of one (1) boom truck, at an anticipated

cost of approximately $350,000.00, for use by the Department of Parks & Recreation’s

(DOPR’s) tree trimming crew. The equipment will replace the 2014 Peterbilt truck

(Equipment No. 204) with 75-foot boom, which has been out of service since September

2025. The new vehicle will be leased for five (5) years, with the Fiscal Year 2026 funding

requirement of an estimated $74,000.00 coming from existing accounts.

G. CLAIMS:

COUNTY COUNCIL MEETING AGENDA FEBRUARY 18, 2026
